title: Explicit conversions of foreach loops should be avoided
description: The foreach statement was introduced in the C# language prior to generics to make it easier to work with the non-generic collections available at that time such as ArrayList. The foreach statements allow you to downcast elements of a collection of Objects to any other type. The problem is that to achieve the cast, the foreach statements silently perform explicit type conversion, which at runtime can result in an InvalidCastException.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

never_trigger_on: Explicit LINQ Cast<T> or OfType<T> methods
logic.reasoning: Detects foreach loops with explicit type conversions from non-generic collections (ArrayList, Hashtable, non-generic IEnumerable). The pattern matches foreach with a specific type (not var, not object) iterating over a collection. Exclusions prevent false positives from generic collections, var usage, and explicit LINQ casting methods which make the intent clear.

should_trigger: foreach (string item in list) { }  // where list is ArrayList
foreach (int number in collection) { }  // where collection is IEnumerable
foreach (MyClass obj in items) { }  // where items is non-generic
